messages.text: Post-mortem for INC-284 is up. TL;DR: OAuth went down for 17 min and we force-logged-out ~3400 enterprise users because authenticateUser hits the provider on every request.
Right, there's no cached session to fall back to. I think we cache sessions in Redis and validate against the cache first, then only hit the provider when there's no cache.
If we keep sessions alive longer we need refresh-token rotation, otherwise we widen the replay window. I don't want to trade an availability bug for a security bug.
Agreed. Plan: Redis session cache + retry/backoff on the provider + fallback to cache + rotate refresh tokens. I'll write it up as ADR-012 and put the change in PR #842.
+1. Please add a regression test that a provider outage does NOT log out a user with a valid cached session.
